relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Joseph brought the family’s arrival in Goshen before Pharaoh.
Five brothers stood before the throne.
They named themselves as shepherds, as their fathers had been.
They asked to sojourn in Goshen because famine had emptied Canaan’s pasture.
Pharaoh acknowledged Joseph’s family.
The best land opened to them, and capable shepherds could serve Pharaoh’s flocks.
Jacob stood before Pharaoh and blessed him.
Pharaoh asked the measure of Jacob’s life.
Jacob called his years few and hard, a sojourner among sojourners.
Jacob blessed Pharaoh again and departed.
Joseph settled his family in the best of Egypt’s land.
Joseph sustained the house of his father with bread.
The famine pressed Egypt and Canaan until the lands languished.
Silver flowed into Pharaoh’s house through grain.
When silver failed, the people cried for bread.
Livestock became the exchange for survival.
Joseph guided them through another year with bread.
When livestock failed, only body and ground remained.
They asked for life, seed, and bread, even at the cost of land.
The land of Egypt became Pharaoh’s through famine pressure.
The people were moved through the cities.
The priests’ land remained outside the purchase.
Joseph gave seed back to the people to sow.
A fifth would go to Pharaoh and four parts would sustain life.
The people received survival as favor.
The fifth became a lasting statute, except for priestly ground.
Israel rooted in Goshen, fruitful and multiplying.
Jacob lived seventeen years in Egypt.
As death neared, Israel asked Joseph for covenant-kindness and truth: do not bury me in Egypt.
He asked to lie with his fathers in their burial place.
Joseph swore, and Israel bowed at the bed’s head.
